content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

April 24, 2017

Blaine R. Cox
Attorney, Oakdale Irrigation District
Darrell Nelson Schrimp
Pallios Pacer & Silva
Attorneys
1601 I Street, Fifth Floor
Modesto, CA 95354

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-17-084

Dear Mr. Cox:

This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of the Oakdale Irrigation District, a California Water District Board of Directors ("Board") regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Section 83114 provides that any person may request an opinion or written advice from the Commission with respect to his or her own duties under the Act. Regulation 18329(b) states that formal written advice may be requested in writing by any person whose duties under the Act are in question or by that person's authorized representative.² Regulation 18329(c) provides that other persons may request informal assistance concerning provisions of the Act such as those with a duty to advise other persons relating to their duties or actions under the Act or by any agency whose members or employees are subject to the provisions of the Act.

You have not indicated that you are seeking advice as the authorized representative of a person whose duties under the Act are in question. Therefore, we must treat your letter as a request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).

Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest or Section 1090.

________________

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3) )

File No. I-17-084
Page No. 2

QUESTION

May Oakdale Irrigation District Board of Directors member Linda Santos ("Director
Santos") participate in the Board's action to file a petition for Writ of Mandamus seeking to halt the
recall election against her?

CONCLUSION

No. Under the Act, Director Santos may not participate in the Board's action to file a
petition for Writ of Mandamus seeking to halt the recall election because there is a reasonably
foreseeable material financial effect upon her interests.

FACTS

In November 2016, members of the public circulated a petition seeking to recall Director
Santos. In December 2016, the Registrar of Voters certified the signatures on the recall petition as
sufficient – with nearly 50% more signatures than necessary to require the recall and the
Irrigation District Board of Directors ("Board") to issue an order directing that the recall election be
held.

In late February/early March 2017, a member of the public alleged that many of the
signatures on the recall petition were obtained by fraud in violation of California Elections Code
Section 18600. The member of the public presented your office with a private investigator's reports,
which contained anonymous accounts from approximately five members of the public regarding
their interaction(s) with individuals circulating the recall petition. Each account alleged that an
individual circulating the petition stated, to some varying degree, that the petition would "stop
water from being sent south" and didn't mention the recall; however, the face of the petition clearly
stated that it was a petition to recall Director Santos. You stated that the Board has ordered further
investigation into the alleged fraud. Once the investigation is complete, the Board will have to
decide whether to take action by filing a Writ of Mandamus to halt the recall election.

You wish to know whether Director Santos has a financial interest for purposes of the Act
regarding the decision of the Board on whether to file a Petition for Writ of Mandamus to halt the
recall. Director Santos receives a salary as compensation for her position on the Board.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using
his or her position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest.
Section 82048 defines "public official" as every member, officer, employee, or consultant of a state
or local government agency, including city councilmembers.

Financial interests from which conflicts of interests may arise are defined in Section 87103
as follows:

• An interest in a business entity in which the official has a direct or indirect investment of
$2,000 or more (Section 87103(a)) or in which the official is a director, officer, partner,
trustee, employee, or holds any position of management (Section 87103(d).)

File No. 1-17-084
Page No. 3

• An interest in real property in which the official has a direct or indirect interest of $2,000 or more. (Section 87103(b).) An indirect real property means a business or real property interest owned by the spouse or dependent child of the official.

• An interest in a source of income to the official, including promised income, which aggregates to $500 or more within 12 months prior to the decision. (Section 87103(c).) "Income" is defined to include any community property interest in the income of a spouse and a pro rata share of the income of any business entity or trust in which the official (or his or her spouse) owns directly, indirectly, or beneficially, a 10-percent or greater interest. (Section 82030(a).)

• An interest in a source of gifts to the official if the gifts aggregate to $470 or more within 12 months prior to the decision. (Section 87103(e).)

• An interest in the official's personal finances, including those of the official's immediate family. This is known as the "personal financial effects" rule. (Section 87103.)

The Act excludes from the definition of "income" any "salary and reimbursement for expenses incurred per diem and social security, or other similar benefit payments received from a state, local, or federal government agency . . . ." (Section 82030(b)(2).) The salary paid by a state, local or federal government agency are generally not considered "income" under Section 82030(b)(2) and Regulation 18232(g).³ However, material financial effects on an official's government salary may still be disqualifying under limited circumstances as a material and foreseeable financial effect on the official's personal finances that is distinct from other officials. (See Boydston Advice Letter, No. A-12-133.) Thus, we continue the analysis based on financial effects to Director Santos' personal finances.

Foreseeability

A public official has a disqualifying conflict of interest in a governmental decision, if the decision will have a reasonably foreseeable and material financial effect on one or more of his or her financial interests. (Section 87103.)

Regulation 18701 provides in pertinent part:

"(a) Financial Interest Explicitly Involved: A financial effect on a financial interest is presumed to be reasonably foreseeable if the financial interest is a named party in, or the subject of, a governmental decision before the official or the official's agency. A financial interest is the subject of a proceeding if the decision involves the issuance, renewal, approval, denial or revocation of any license, permit, or other entitlement to, or contract with, the financial interest, and includes any governmental decision affecting a real property financial interest as described in Regulation 18702.2(a)(1) - (6)."

³ Therefore Director Santos will not have an interest in the Board as a source of income.

File No. I-17-084
Page No. 4

In this case Director Santos' position is the subject of the recall and the potential
writ to be filed would be focused on deciding whether or not to halt the recall election of
Director Santos which would determine whether or not she would be permitted to keep
her compensated position on the Board. Therefore, her position and in turn her personal
finances are the subject of this governmental decision.

Materiality

Regulation 18702.5 provides the appropriate materiality standard for personal financial
effects:

"(a) A personal financial effect means the financial effect of a governmental
decision on the personal finances of a public official or his or her immediate
family. The financial effect is material if the official or the official's immediate
family member will receive a measurable financial benefit or loss from the
decision.
(b) Notwithstanding subdivision (a), a personal financial effect does not
include:
(1) Any establishment of or change to benefits provided under an employment
or retirement policy for employees or retirees if the financial effect of the decision
applies equally to all employees in the same bargaining unit or other
representative group . . . ."

In this case Director Santos will receive a measurable loss if the recall effort moves forward
and she loses her position on the Board, and this change does not affect any other board member,
only herself. Therefore, there is a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect upon her personal
finances and she would have a conflict of interest in participating in the decision to begin the
process of halting the recall election by filing a Writ of Mandamus.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Hyla P. Wagner
General Counsel

By: Sukhi K. Brar
Counsel, Legal Division

SKB:jgl

• 
An interest in real property in which the official has a direct or indirect interest of $2,000 or 
more. (Section 87103(b).) An indirect interest in business or real property means a business 
or real property interest owned by the spouse or dependent child of the official. 
•

(Section 82030( a).)

months prior to the decision. (Section 87103(e).)

with, the financial interest, and includes any governmental decision affecting a

3 Therefore Director Santos will not have an interest in the Board as a source of income.

recall election against her?

CONCLUSION: No. Under the Act, Director Santos may not participate in the Board's action to file a

foreseeable material financial effect upon her interests.
